skill.name: appinsights-instrumentation
skill.description: Guidance for planning Azure Application Insights instrumentation for ASP.NET Core, Node.js, and Python applications. Includes setup references, telemetry patterns, Azure Monitor SDK notes, and cloud configuration guidance.
skill.summary: Plan Application Insights instrumentation for Azure-hosted web applications.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, path traversal, command execution, network, and environment access patterns. Manual review found the weak cryptography and path traversal hits are false positives from Application Insights terminology and relative documentation links. Remaining issues are low-risk operational guidance for Azure CLI commands and Application Insights connection string configuration, with no evidence of prompt injection or malicious exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Azure CLI and Package Installation Guidance
low_findings.description: The static command-execution findings are documentation examples for installing Azure Monitor packages and configuring Application Insights. They are legitimate for this skill, but users should review commands before execution because they can affect dependencies or cloud configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are static package installation examples for known Azure Monitor libraries. Risk is limited to operational changes if an assistant executes them without user approval.

low_findings.title: Application Insights Connection String Handling
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to set APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING as an environment variable. This is expected for Application Insights, but the value is sensitive configuration and should not be committed or printed in logs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines show placeholder connection string usage, not exfiltration. The security concern is secret handling hygiene around a real production value.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives Confirmed
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography detections are keyword matches against Application Insights text, and path traversal detections are relative links to local reference material. No cryptographic implementation or filesystem traversal behavior was found in the reviewed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these lines contain product terminology, Bicep parameter descriptions, and local documentation links. They do not implement weak cryptography or filesystem access.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent telemetry guidance before changing production applications. This skill explains Azure Application Insights setup patterns, SDK choices, and configuration steps for common web stacks.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use auto-instrumentation for Azure App Service applications.
Provides setup guidance for ASP.NET Core, Node.js, and Python telemetry libraries.
References Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry distro and exporter options.
Shows how to create an Application Insights resource with Bicep guidance.
Describes connection string configuration through environment variables.
limitations: Does not orchestrate complete application changes by itself.
Focuses on Azure-hosted web applications and common SDK paths.
Requires users to supply Azure subscription, resource group, and app details.
Does not validate live Azure permissions, costs, or deployment state.

use_cases.title: Plan ASP.NET Core Monitoring
use_cases.description: Review the recommended Azure Monitor package and configuration steps before changing an ASP.NET Core service.

use_cases.title: Choose Auto-Instrumentation
use_cases.description: Determine whether an Azure App Service application can use portal-based Application Insights setup without code changes.

use_cases.title: Standardize Telemetry Guidance
use_cases.description: Create consistent observability instructions for teams using Application Insights across Node.js and Python services.

prompt_templates.title: Identify the App Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me determine whether my web application should use Application Insights auto-instrumentation or SDK instrumentation.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare SDK Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Give me Application Insights instrumentation guidance for a Node.js app hosted in Azure App Service.

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Telemetry Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned Application Insights setup for an ASP.NET Core service and identify missing configuration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Compare OpenTelemetry Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry distro and exporter options for a Python service with custom logging needs.

output_examples.input: I have an ASP.NET Core app in Azure App Service. How should I add Application Insights?
output_examples.output: A short recommendation explaining auto-instrumentation eligibility, the SDK fallback path, and where to store the connection string.

output_examples.input: What should my Node.js service change before sending telemetry to Azure Monitor?
output: Install the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry package.
Initialize telemetry early in the entry file.
Provide the Application Insights connection string through environment configuration.

output_examples.input: Which Application Insights resource setup should I use with infrastructure as code?
output_examples.output: A concise plan covering a Log Analytics workspace, an Application Insights component, and environment configuration for the hosted app.

best_practices: Confirm the application language, framework, and hosting model before recommending an instrumentation path.
Store connection strings in environment configuration or managed platform settings, not source files.
Use Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry guidance that matches the application runtime and deployment model.
anti_patterns: Do not commit Application Insights connection strings to repository files.
Do not assume auto-instrumentation works outside supported Azure App Service scenarios.
Do not execute Azure CLI commands without confirming the target subscription and resource group.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my application automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and reference steps. Another workflow should handle direct project changes.

faq.question: Which application stacks are covered?
faq.answer: The guidance covers ASP.NET Core, Node.js, Python, and selected Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry references.

faq.question: Can it help with Azure App Service auto-instrumentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when portal-based auto-instrumentation is appropriate for supported Azure App Service apps.

faq.question: Does it create Azure resources?
faq.answer: It includes resource creation guidance, but users must review and run any Azure commands themselves.

faq.question: How should connection strings be handled?
faq.answer: Use environment variables or managed app settings. Avoid storing connection strings in source-controlled files.

faq.question: Does it replace Azure Monitor documentation?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes common paths and SDK references for AI-assisted planning.
